creator: collin.funk1@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to Gleb Popov from comment #3)
I'm not too experienced with protobuf. I took a quick look at their release page again and they mark breaking changes. 3.22.2 -> 3.25.3 seems to be pretty safe. Unless I missed something, Java doesn't seem to have any breaking changes. If you look at the 3.26 RC's you can see how breaking changes are marked.

mysql-connector-j is version 8.1.0 in main right now. From what I could find, version 8.0.32 bumped the dependency to protobuf-java 3.21.9 [1].
mysql-connector-j version 8.3.0 bumped it to protobuf-java 3.25.1 [2]. Whenever the maintainer updates it I assume they will probably run into build problems and have to update this first. Hopefully that helps you know about potential breakage. :)

[1] https://github.com/mysql/mysql-connector-j/blob/805f872a57875f311cb82487efcfb070411a3fa0/CHANGES#L108C54-L108C74
[2] https://github.com/mysql/mysql-connector-j/blame/805f872a57875f311cb82487efcfb070411a3fa0/CHANGES#L15
